chosen_angle: How a mother's grief rewrote military history
cold_open: They changed the report. Crossed out pilot error. Wrote reasons unknown. That doesn't happen in the Navy.

hook.opening_scene: A Navy clerk crossing out words on an official document
hook.opening_question: Why did the Navy change its own conclusion?

act1.title: The Crime Scene
act1.summary: December 5 1945. Flight 19 departs on a routine navigation exercise.
act1.cliffhanger: Taylor refuses to switch to emergency frequency

act2.title: Rewinding the Clock
act2.summary: The search that vanished too. A rescue plane with 13 crew disappears.

act3.title: The Why
act3.summary: Taylor's mother campaigns to clear his name. The Navy changes its verdict.

ending.reframe: The real mystery is not what happened but who controls the story
ending.final_line: The ocean keeps what it takes. But the records — those can be rewritten.
ending.cta: If this changed how you see history, subscribe.

pov_shift.line: To the Navy, this was a closed case. To one mother in Florida, it was an open wound.

archival_moment: Taylor's last known words: 'We'll fly west until we hit the beach or run out of gas'
pacing_notes: Open slow, accelerate through Act 2, hold silence after reveal
